creator: bf2006a@yahoo.com
text:
--- On Sat, 3/14/09, Mark Linimon <linimon@lonesome.com> wrote:

At the moment, I don't have a place to put it, so I'll truncate
the larger of the two attachments, and use bzip2 -- if someone
wants the entire transcript, they can contact me via email and
I'll send it to them.  I've already sent it to the other two
that you cc'ed.

Mark: In the future, what size limit should I observe?

Here's the text of the rejected message:



My follow-up, which I am forwarding directly to the lists,
after being rejected by the spam filter.

I repeat for the benefit of the classifier: this is _not_ a
problem associated with any one port, or the ports system as a
whole, but tmpfs-related changes in the base system. No panic
was induced (but see my kern/132597), just build failures.

b.

--- On Sat, 3/14/09, bf <bf2006a@yahoo.com> wrote:

> From: bf <bf2006a@yahoo.com>
> Subject: Re: kern/132620: widespread failure of port builds on recent 8-CURRENT with tmpfs WRKDIR
> To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org
> Date: Saturday, March 14, 2009, 4:12 AM
> The build transcripts. /tmp is a tmpfs mounted at boot via
> the
> following line in /etc/fstab:
>
> tmpfs     /tmp     tmpfs     rw     0     0
>
> WRKDIRPREFIX=/tmp (see ports(4) and
> /usr/ports/Mk/bsd.port.mk .)
>
> I should add that these errors are repeatable only in the
> sense
> that I can be fairly sure that one will occur during a
> sequence
> of port builds, and that, once such an error has occurred
> after
> 'make install', if I then quickly issue 'make
> clean' and then
> 'make install' again, the same error will recur.
> However, note
> that the two attached build transcripts are of successive,
> but
> slightly different attempts to install shells/bash, and
> slightly
> different failures occur at different points in the
> sequence of
> builds.
>
> Regards,
>            b.
